domain: acoustic mic-array DOA, SECOND independent corpus (AV16.3) -- within-domain robustness vs LOCATA
harness: experiments/av163_flip.py (imports compress3 from rehab033.py -- bit-identical matched-bit three-arm compressor)
dataset: AV16.3 (Lathoud, Odobez, Gatica-Perez, MLMI'04; CC-BY, cite the paper). Independent of LOCATA: different array, room, speakers. 5 single-speaker (1p) sequences from the open glat.info host.
array: AV16.3 array1: 8-mic uniform CIRCULAR array (r=0.1 m; mic coords from corpus README formula), 16 kHz
consumer: incoherent wideband MUSIC generalised to the 2D circular geometry (single source per 0.75 s window; moving speaker)
ground_truth: the corpus's camera-tracked 3D-MOUTH LOCATION (max err 1.2 cm) -> ABSOLUTE azimuth at the array-1 centre. The sealed D2 protocol ('tracked source DOA') is HONORED -- NO reference amendment (unlike D1: the 8-mic array resolves azimuth and the tracked-GT cross-check is strong).
split: calibration = {seq01, seq02}; held-out = DISJOINT {seq03, seq11, seq15} (a real disjoint-recording held-out)
verdict: CONFIRMED -- all three sealed bars pass against the ABSOLUTE tracked GT on DISJOINT held-out recordings. The consumer-relative flip (angle-favoring code beats reconstruction-optimal Lloyd-Max at matched bits on the downstream DOA task, while Lloyd reconstructs the raw signal better -- 201/201 recon-trade) replicates on a SECOND, fully independent acoustic corpus. Config frozen + sealed before the confirm; no reference amendment (sealed protocol honored).
